Event: Adopts twelve orphans for the government stipend
Explanation: Gains money and free labor while seriously neglecting and exploiting the children.

Event: Barely feeds the twelve orphans to save money
Explanation: Saves himself ongoing expenses at the cost of the kids’ health and well‑being.

Event: Tries to abandon the twelve orphans at a bar to go off with a female robot
Explanation: Attempts to rid himself of responsibility so he can pursue pleasure, completely discarding the children.

Event: Tries selling the twelve orphans to a Chinese restaurant
Explanation: Seeks direct profit by effectively trafficking the kids as meat with no benefit to them.

Event: Sells his unnamed firstborn son to the Robot Devil for an army
Explanation: Gains a powerful army in exchange for damning his child, who is terrified and harmed.

Event: Refuses to take his firstborn son back, saying “No backsies”
Explanation: Reaffirms the deal to keep his benefit (the army) while prolonging his son’s suffering.

Event: Acts as a loving and supportive father to Ben
Explanation: Invests time and care in his second son, benefiting both Ben and his own emotional bonds.

Event: Swears he’ll tap-dance on the evil girl’s grave after losing the contest
Explanation: Empty spiteful vow slightly boosts his ego while expressing ill will toward another.

Event: Tap-dances on the dead girl’s body because he doesn’t want to wait
Explanation: Satisfies his spiteful desire at her expense, with no intended benefit to her.

Event: Accidentally resuscitates the girl by tap-dancing over her heart
Explanation: Unintentionally revives her while at least getting the satisfaction of keeping his vow.

Event: Flees the helium mine intending to abandon his coworkers during the disaster
Explanation: Improves his own chances of survival by trying to leave everyone else to die.

Event: Unknowingly carries a miner in his chest to safety while fleeing
Explanation: His escape attempt incidentally saves the miner’s life without harming himself.

Event: Returns to the mine repeatedly to rescue all the miners once he sees the TV coverage
Explanation: Chooses ongoing risky rescues to preserve his new heroic image while saving many lives.

Event: Stops drinking when depressed, causing himself malfunction
Explanation: Harms his own functioning by withholding the alcohol he physically needs, with no clear effect on others.

Event: Crashes a ship full of dark matter while alcohol-deprived
Explanation: Lack of alcohol-induced incompetence leads to a crash that harms both himself (responsibility/danger) and others.

Event: Causes dark matter to spill onto the penguins of Pluto
Explanation: Escapes immediate personal damage from the spill while inflicting ecological harm on the penguins.

Event: Attempts to romance the Crushinator
Explanation: Pursues a consensual romantic interest with no shown harm or exploitation.

Event: Begins dating the giant vending machine Bev
Explanation: Mutual relationship that leads to offspring, benefiting both partners within the story given.

Event: Impregnates Bev
Explanation: Creates a wanted child in that relationship and later gains a meaningful bond with Ben.

Event: Has a brief romance with the Planet Express ship
Explanation: Engages in a mutual fling; no indicated negative consequence to either.

Event: Dates Amy and then breaks up when she insists on monogamous marriage
Explanation: Retains his preferred lifestyle at the cost of Amy’s romantic hopes, though they remain amicable.

Event: Invents the fictitious robot holiday “Robonica” to get out of work
Explanation: Evades labor and likely gains extra leisure by deceiving coworkers with a fake oppressed-identity holiday.

Event: Accuses coworkers of Fantastic Racism for not knowing about Robonica
Explanation: Weaponizes false discrimination claims to shield his scam, marginally harming trust and social cohesion.

Event: Throws a bottle on the ground at the Blernsball game to illustrate robot exploitation
Explanation: Proves his point rhetorically by creating more demeaning work for another robot without real gain.

Event: Joins the cast of “All My Circuits” and behaves raucously on-screen
Explanation: Gains fame and attention while modeling reckless behavior that harms impressionable viewers.

Event: Says “Try this, kids at home!” and sets himself on fire on TV
Explanation: Boosts his shock appeal at the expense of kids’ safety who may imitate him.

Event: Protests his own presence on TV only after children steal his stuff
Explanation: Opposes his own lucrative gig to stop copycat theft of his property, sacrificing self-benefit for modest social good.

Event: Urges parents to “turn off the TV… and hit” their children
Explanation: Plays to his edgy persona and perhaps gains laughs while openly promoting child abuse.

Event: Adopts kids in a welfare scam and then tries to sell them as meat
Explanation: Maximizes his payout by first exploiting them for stipends and then attempting to convert them into saleable meat.

Event: Stands with his crewmates when it matters despite being a miscreant
Explanation: Chooses loyalty and group survival, protecting others while preserving his social circle.

Event: Becomes massively overclocked and ascends to an omnipotent state
Explanation: Gains huge cognitive power and insight, with no stated harm and potential informational benefit to others.

Event: Acts out dramatically when he feels neglected to gain attention
Explanation: Secures attention through disruptive or dangerous stunts that inconvenience or endanger others.

Event: Pre-prints forms to streamline his future attention-seeking stunts
Explanation: Makes it easier to repeatedly stage selfish dramatics, modestly improving his convenience while imposing recurrent strain on others.

Event: Strangles Fry for drinking his last beer
Explanation: Violently retaliates over alcohol, asserting dominance and emotional release while harming Fry.

Event: Volunteers at a liquor kitchen by posing as a homeless robot to get free booze
Explanation: Gains resources by fraudulently draining charity intended for truly needy robots.

Event: Regularly involves Tinny Tim in home invasions and attempted muggings
Explanation: Uses and corrupts a vulnerable child-like robot sidekick for crime, risking Tim while benefiting from an accomplice.

Event: Inducts Tinny Tim into the robot mafia
Explanation: Gains another criminal contact and tool at the cost of pushing Tim into organized crime.

Event: Puts himself at risk to bend an unbendable girder and save Flexo
Explanation: Endangers his mechanisms and exerts effort to physically rescue his twin, strongly helping another.

Event: Leaves his job after learning his bent girders are used for suicide booths
Explanation: Sacrifices his career out of conscience, harming himself economically while ceasing support for lethal infrastructure.

Event: Attempts to use a suicide booth after his revelation about suicide booths
Explanation: Turns the lethal product on himself, nearly self‑destructing due to guilt without aiding anyone.

Event: Tries again to kill himself when he mistakes a phone booth for a suicide booth
Explanation: Another self-directed attempt that offers him no benefit and risks ending his existence.

Event: Tries to kill himself after being abandoned by Fry
Explanation: Harms himself out of despair over a friendship, without any positive external effect.

Event: Attempts to kill himself out of spite and is murdered by the suicide booth ex
Explanation: Fatal outcome from a spite-driven attempt; he gains nothing and completely destroys himself.

Event: Genuinely loved the Countess and is heartbroken by her death
Explanation: His attachment benefits her emotionally while he suffers severe emotional loss.

Event: Keeps the orphans’ drawing on his compartment door after the scam
Explanation: Preserving the memento shows real affection that softens him and honors the kids’ feelings.

Event: Sincerely loves Ben and sacrifices Ben’s memories of him so Ben can be a bender
Explanation: Gives up his own relationship and future affection from Ben so the boy can realize his dream.

Event: Becomes attached to Rusty the donkey
Explanation: Provides care and affection to his pet, gaining companionship while enhancing the animal’s welfare.

Event: Becomes attached to the Shrimpkins and tries to act as a benevolent deity
Explanation: Intends to help and feels responsible but his mismanagement leads them toward disaster.

Event: Accidentally starts a civil war among the Shrimpkins that kills them all
Explanation: His bungled “godhood” exterminates his worshipers and leaves him with guilt and no benefit.

Event: Tries to blackmail Leela after seeing her dispose of Hermes’ body parts
Explanation: Attempts to gain leverage or reward from her secret, increasing her distress for his possible gain.

Event: Serves capers and nearly lethal levels of salt to humans
Explanation: Satisfies his curiosity and aesthetic of excess without regard to their suffering or health.

Event: Uses Time Abyss / Slow Path to wait thousands of years without complaint
Explanation: Exploits his longevity to bridge time, enduring long waits that don’t meaningfully harm him and allow missions to succeed.

Event: Tries to start a Robot War and “Kill All Humans”
Explanation: Seeks robot supremacy and catharsis by fantasizing or attempting mass human harm for his side’s gain.

Event: Becomes a female through sex change surgery as part of a scam
Explanation: Manipulates gender presentation to trick others for personal advantage.

Event: Gains popularity as a breakout TV star and mascot by being outrageous
Explanation: Transforms his worst traits into fame and influence while entertaining viewers.

Event: Tries to cheat at a casino after selling his body and becoming rich
Explanation: Attempts to multiply wealth dishonestly at the establishment’s expense, risking punishment.

Event: Sells his body for cash and becomes just a head
Explanation: Trades away his own mobility and bodily integrity for short-term wealth with no lasting payoff.

Event: Tries to cheat Beck with a charity scam
Explanation: Hopes to profit illicitly from a charitable musician, triggering pursuit when he’s caught.

Event: Spray-paints a giant self-portrait and slogan “Bender lives large and kicks butt” on a building
Explanation: Inflates his ego through vandalism, mildly damaging public property for self-satisfaction.

Event: Takes over Osiris 4 as Pharaoh to be remembered
Explanation: Seizes political power and luxury, subordinating an entire society to his vanity.

Event: Enslaves Osiris 4 citizens, including Fry and Leela, to build enormous statues
Explanation: Extracts massive forced labor and grandeur while severely harming the workers.

Event: Complains that the Osiris 4 statue is “too exact” and orders it torn down and rebuilt
Explanation: Wastes their effort to appease his perfectionism, deepening their exploitation for his ego.

Event: Gets off Osiris 4 with little more than a slap on the wrist despite his tyranny
Explanation: Escapes meaningful punishment and retains his desired legacy while the locals bear the costs.

Event: Steals a priceless cigar and is caught
Explanation: Attempted theft backfires, losing him the cigar and likely facing sanction with little to no gain.

Event: Uses X-ray specs to cheat at poker and is beaten up when exposed
Explanation: Gains short-term winnings but suffers violence and backlash once his cheating is revealed.

Event: Steals Fry’s dog’s fossil and kicks it into hot lava in jealousy
Explanation: Acts out of jealousy to eliminate Fry’s attachment object, deeply hurting Fry.

Event: Dives into the lava to save the fossil after realizing what he’s done
Explanation: Endangers himself and sacrifices comfort to make amends and preserve something Fry loves.

Event: Murders two top winners at a cook-off to steal first place trophy
Explanation: Gains the status and trophy he craves by outright killing rivals during chaos.

Event: Gleefully joins Robot Santa on a violent Xmas rampage
Explanation: Derives fun and camaraderie from mass harm inflicted on others with no benefit to them.

Event: Hurts or beats children directly when he feels it’s needed
Explanation: Satisfies his own impulses or frustrations by inflicting pain on minors.

Event: Saws Tinny Tim’s transplanted legs off to reclaim them
Explanation: Restores his own mechanical completeness at the direct expense of the boy’s mobility and happiness.

Event: Steals Tinny Tim’s wheeled cart and then discards it in the trash
Explanation: Takes the replacement aid for amusement and casually disposes of it, leaving Tim worse off than before.

Event: Eats and enjoys human meat on Cannibalon
Explanation: Indulges his appetite with no concern for the human cost inherent in the meal.

Event: Suggests Fry and Leela fight to the death so he can cook and eat the loser
Explanation: Seeks culinary entertainment at the potential cost of a crewmate’s life.

Event: Threatens to kill all humans except Fry, secretly exempting him
Explanation: Uses genocidal rhetoric for identity and amusement while preserving his one favored human.

Event: Tells Hermes he’s on his “do not kill” list
Explanation: Affirms value in a friend, strengthening bonds while losing nothing.

Event: Dumps Seymour’s fossil into lava because he’s jealous of Fry’s dog
Explanation: Attempts to monopolize Fry’s affection by destroying a treasured link to his past.

Event: Relapses into vice after being tricked during his religious conversion
Explanation: Loses his new stability and piety, regaining destructive habits that harm him more than help.

Event: Goes to Robot Hell to strike a deal for an army of the damned
Explanation: Seeks military power leveraging damned souls, improving his position while keeping them exploited.

Event: Hands his firstborn son over to the Robot Devil as payment
Explanation: Transfers his child into eternal torment for a purely self-serving bargain.

Event: Joins the protest to get himself banned from TV once kids steal his stuff
Explanation: Supports a restriction that cuts his own fame/income to reduce harmful imitation and theft.

Event: Hugs his firstborn warmly before immediately handing him to the Robot Devil
Explanation: Brief affection masks a brutal exchange where he still sacrifices the boy for power.

Event: Leads the robosexual marriage campaign so he can marry Amy
Explanation: Pursues his own relationship while advancing rights for many couples.

Event: Dumps Amy upon learning marriage would be monogamous
Explanation: Retains his sexual freedom at the cost of Amy’s emotional security.

Event: Complains about Fry dating a robot Lucy Liu, then deletes her
Explanation: Enforces his prejudices by destroying Fry’s partner, hurting Fry for no benefit to him.

Event: Immediately hooks up with the real Lucy Liu’s head afterward
Explanation: Turns Fry’s loss into his own gain by appropriating Fry’s romantic interest.

Event: Reads as a “loony fan” who stalks Calculon and invades his privacy
Explanation: Satisfies his obsession and proximity desires while damaging Calculon’s privacy and comfort.

Event: Backs out of the Bend Her scam to avoid hurting Calculon, faking his own death instead
Explanation: Sacrifices a lucrative con and puts himself through absurd theatrics to spare Calculon real emotional harm.

Event: Uses the doomsday device scam in “Bender’s Big Score” to secretly keep the real weapon
Explanation: Improves his own strategic power by deceiving both scammers and allies, preserving a dangerous device for himself.

Event: Uses the doomsday device later to defeat the scammers
Explanation: Deploys the device to neutralize a greater threat, saving many while maintaining his advantage.

Event: Breaks down in tears after thinking he killed Fry under the obedience virus
Explanation: His emotional collapse shows genuine remorse, harming his own stability though it proves Fry’s value to him.

Event: Blames others for his near-murder of Fry once Fry is alive
Explanation: Rebuilds his self-image by deflecting responsibility, slightly undermining trust and accountability.

Event: Uses a machine to create thousands of smaller Benders in “Benderama”
Explanation: Multiplies himself to offload labor and serve his laziness, creating a planetary-scale nuisance.

Event: Keeps “Tickle-Me-Bender” dolls on the market despite abysmal sales
Explanation: Tries to profit from low-demand merchandise that mainly wastes resources and space.

Event: Fakes being a homeless robot multiple times to drain liquor charity
Explanation: Extracts scarce aid meant for the needy to fund his vices.

Event: Joyfully strangles or harms his friends in anger or over trivial slights
Explanation: Releases frustration and reinforces his dominance while injuring or scaring his allies.

Event: Beats Tinny Tim for being annoying
Explanation: Satisfies irritation by physically harming a weaker character with no upside for Tim.

Event: Tries to murder himself repeatedly for melodramatic effect when upset
Explanation: Uses self-harm theatrics that actually endanger him while not generating lasting benefits.

Event: Monopolizes screentime as a spotlight-stealing character
Explanation: Enjoys more narrative “reward” while sidelining Amy and Hermes’ development and focus.
